title: Cyclosorus berroi
intro: Cyclosorus berroi is a species of fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. Traditionally, Cyclosorus berroi is used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus berroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus berroi is often prepared as a decoction, infusate, or tincture to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus berroi is a species of fern. Cyclosorus berroi belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. This species is classified as a member of the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns that are native to various parts of the world. The classification of Cyclosorus berroi is further supported by its characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Cyclosorus berroi.
